entry.title: Understanding A2A Protocol: Agent-to-Agent Communication and Agent Discovery
entry.content:
Understanding A2A Protocol: Agent-to-Agent Communication

In the rapidly evolving landscape of AI systems, Agent2Agent (A2A) Protocol emerges as a critical standard for enabling seamless communication and collaboration between AI agents. This article explores what A2A is, how agent discovery works, and how it complements other protocols like the Model Context Protocol (MCP).

What is A2A?

The A2A protocol is an open standard developed by the Linux Foundation that enables standardized communication and collaboration between AI agents. Unlike traditional approaches where agents are wrapped as tools, A2A allows agents to interact directly as autonomous entities, regardless of their underlying framework or vendor.

The Problem A2A Solves

Consider a practical scenario: planning an international trip. This task requires orchestrating multiple specialized agents:

graph TD    subgraph before["Without A2A: Separate Concerns"]        UA1["Flight Agent"]        UA2["Hotel Agent"]        UA3["Currency Agent"]        UA4["Tour Agent"]        UA1 -.->|custom code| UA2        UA1 -.->|custom code| UA3        UA1 -.->|custom code| UA4        UA2 -.->|custom code| UA3        UA3 -.->|custom code| UA4        style UA1 fill:#ffcccc        style UA2 fill:#ffcccc        style UA3 fill:#ffcccc        style UA4 fill:#ffcccc    end        subgraph after["With A2A: Standardized Collaboration"]        CA1["Flight Agent"]        CA2["Hotel Agent"]        CA3["Currency Agent"]        CA4["Tour Agent"]        CORE["A2A Protocol<br/>Standardized Communication"]        CA1 -->|A2A| CORE        CA2 -->|A2A| CORE        CA3 -->|A2A| CORE        CA4 -->|A2A| CORE        CORE --> CA1        CORE --> CA2        CORE --> CA3        CORE --> CA4        style CA1 fill:#ccffcc        style CA2 fill:#ccffcc        style CA3 fill:#ccffcc        style CA4 fill:#ccffcc        style CORE fill:#cce5ff    end        before -.->|Unified Standard| after
  • A flight booking agent
  • A hotel reservation agent
  • A currency conversion agent
  • A local tour recommendations agent

Without A2A, integrating these diverse agents presents significant challenges:

  1. Agent Exposure: Developers often wrap agents as tools, limiting their capabilities and preventing direct agent-to-agent negotiation
  2. Custom Integrations: Each interaction requires bespoke, point-to-point solutions
  3. Scalability Issues: Systems become difficult to scale and maintain as the number of agents grows
  4. Interoperability Gaps: This limits the organic formation of complex AI ecosystems
  5. Security Concerns: Ad hoc communication lacks consistent security measures

How A2A Solves These Problems

A2A addresses these challenges by establishing a standardized protocol for agents to:

  • Interact reliably and securely
  • Discover each other’s capabilities
  • Communicate without exposing internal implementation details
  • Support complex, multi-turn conversations and negotiations
  • Maintain autonomy while collaborating

Core Benefits of A2A

  1. Secure Collaboration: HTTPS-based communication with consistent security measures
  2. Interoperability: Breaks down silos between different AI agent ecosystems
  3. Agent Autonomy: Allows agents to retain their capabilities while collaborating
  4. Reduced Integration Complexity: Standardized communication reduces engineering overhead
  5. Support for Long-Running Operations: Native support for async execution and streaming

Key Design Principles

A2A follows enterprise-grade design principles:

  • Simplicity: Leverages existing standards like HTTP, JSON-RPC, and Server-Sent Events (SSE)
  • Enterprise Readiness: Robust authentication, authorization, security, privacy, tracing, and monitoring
  • Asynchronous: Native support for long-running tasks and streaming
  • Modality Independent: Agents can communicate using various content types
  • Opaque Execution: Agents collaborate without exposing internal logic, preserving IP and security

Agent Discovery: How Agents Find Each Other

For A2A-enabled agents to collaborate, they first need to discover each other and understand their capabilities. This is where the Agent Card comes in.

The Agent Card: A Digital Business Card for Agents

The Agent Card is a JSON document that serves as a standardized descriptor for an A2A Server (remote agent). It contains:

  • Identity: Name, description, and provider information
  • Service Endpoint: The URL for the A2A service
  • A2A Capabilities: Supported features like streaming or push notifications
  • Authentication: Required security schemes (e.g., Bearer, OAuth2)
  • Skills: Descriptions of available tasks with input/output modes and examples

Discovery Strategies

A2A defines three main discovery strategies:

graph TB    Client["Client Agent<br/>Needs to discover agents"]        subgraph s1["Well-Known URI<br/>Public Discovery"]        Server1["Remote Agent Server"]        Card1["Agent Card<br/>/.well-known/<br/>agent-card.json"]        Server1 --> Card1        Client -->|GET| Card1        style Card1 fill:#ccffff        style Server1 fill:#e6f3ff    end        subgraph s2["Curated Registry<br/>Enterprise Discovery"]        Registry["Central Registry"]        Cards["Agent Card<br/>Database"]        Registry --> Cards        Client -->|Query by Skill| Registry        Registry -->|Return matching| Client        style Registry fill:#ffe6cc        style Cards fill:#fff5e6    end        subgraph s3["Direct Config<br/>Private Discovery"]        Config["Config File<br/>Environment<br/>Variables"]        Client -->|Read| Config        style Config fill:#f0f0f0    end        Client -.-> s1    Client -.-> s2    Client -.-> s3

1. Well-Known URI (Public Discovery)

Best for: Public agents or domain-specific discovery

Mechanism:

  • A2A Servers host their Agent Card at a standardized location: https://{agent-server-domain}/.well-known/agent-card.json
  • Client agents perform an HTTP GET request to this endpoint
  • The server returns the Agent Card as a JSON response

Advantages:

  • Easy to implement
  • Follows RFC 8615 standards
  • Facilitates automated discovery

2. Curated Registries (Catalog-Based Discovery)

Best for: Enterprise environments or public marketplaces

Mechanism:

  • A central registry service maintains a collection of Agent Cards
  • A2A Servers publish their cards to the registry
  • Client agents query the registry by skills, tags, or other criteria

Advantages:

  • Centralized management and governance
  • Capability-based discovery
  • Support for access controls and trust frameworks

3. Direct Configuration (Private Discovery)

Best for: Tightly coupled systems or development environments

Mechanism:

  • Client applications are directly configured with Agent Card information through config files or environment variables

Advantages:

  • Straightforward for known, static relationships

Disadvantages:

  • Inflexible for dynamic scenarios
  • Requires client reconfiguration for updates

Securing Agent Cards

Since Agent Cards may contain sensitive information, A2A recommends:

  • Authenticated Agent Cards: Use authenticated extended versions for sensitive data
  • Secure Endpoints: Implement access controls via mTLS, IP restrictions, or OAuth 2.0
  • Registry Selective Disclosure: Return different cards based on client identity and permissions
  • No Static Secrets: Use out-of-band dynamic credentials instead

A2A and MCP: Complementary Protocols for Agentic Systems

While A2A enables agent-to-agent collaboration, the Model Context Protocol (MCP) focuses on how agents interact with tools and resources. These protocols are complementary, not competing.

graph TB    subgraph A2A["A2A Protocol<br/>Agent-to-Agent Collaboration <br/    > "]        Agent1["Agent A<br/>Autonomous System<br/>Can reason, plan<br/>Multi-turn dialogue"]        Agent2["Agent B<br/>Autonomous System<br/>Can reason, plan<br/>Multi-turn dialogue"]        Agent1 <-->|Negotiate<br/>Delegate Tasks| Agent2        style Agent1 fill:#ccffff        style Agent2 fill:#ccffff    end        subgraph MCP["MCP Protocol<br/>Agent-to-Tool Integration"]        Agent["Agent<br/>Intelligent System"]        Tool1["Calculator"]        Tool2["Database API"]        Tool3["Weather Service"]        Agent -->|Call| Tool1        Agent -->|Call| Tool2        Agent -->|Call| Tool3        style Agent fill:#ffffcc        style Tool1 fill:#f0f0f0        style Tool2 fill:#f0f0f0        style Tool3 fill:#f0f0f0    end        A2A -->|Complementary<br/>Protocols| MCP        Note["<b>Key Difference:</b><br/>A2A: Agents partnering on tasks<br/>MCP: Agents using capabilities"]    style Note fill:#fff,stroke:#000,stroke-width:2px

Understanding the Difference

Model Context Protocol (MCP) is designed for:

  • Connecting AI models and agents to tools, APIs, and external resources
  • Standardizing how agents invoke stateless, well-defined functions
  • Typical use cases: LLM calling an external API, agent querying a database

Agent2Agent Protocol (A2A) is designed for:

  • Enabling independent agents to collaborate with each other
  • Supporting complex, multi-turn conversations and negotiations
  • Typical use cases: Customer service agent delegating to billing agent, travel agent coordinating with multiple service agents

The Key Distinction

  • MCP: Agents using capabilities (tools)
  • A2A: Agents partnering on tasks

Comprehensive Comparison: API vs MCP vs A2A

Here’s a detailed comparison of the three approaches:

CharacteristicDirect API CallMCP (Tool Protocol)A2A (Agent Protocol)
Main ActorDeveloper (you)AI Model (seeking tools)Two AI Agents (collaborating/trading)
Communication GoalGet data or resultsAccess specific local/private resourcesComplete complex, cross-entity intentions
FlexibilityLow (hardcoded code)Medium (standard interface, plug-and-play)High (autonomous decision-making, dynamic interaction)
Blockchain Use CaseSimple Oracle price feedsAI reads wallet private ledgerIntent-driven asset settlement

This comparison shows why A2A is particularly powerful for complex scenarios like intent-driven blockchain applications, where multiple autonomous agents need to negotiate and collaborate without pre-determined logic.

Real-World Example: The Auto Repair Shop

Imagine an autonomous auto repair shop with AI agent “mechanics”:

graph TB    Customer["Customer<br/>My car is making a rattling noise"]        subgraph UserAgent["User-Agent (A2A)"]        Manager["Shop Manager"]    end        subgraph AgentAgent["Agent-Agent (A2A)"]        Mechanic["Mechanic"]        Supplier["Parts Supplier"]    end        subgraph Tools["Agent-Tool (MCP)"]        Scanner["Vehicle Scanner"]        Manual["Repair Manual DB"]        Lift["Platform Lift"]    end        Customer -->|A2A dialogue<br/>Multi-turn negotiation| Manager        Manager -->|A2A<br/>Diagnostic questions| Mechanic        Mechanic -->|A2A<br/>Check inventory| Supplier    Supplier -->|A2A<br/>Provide quote| Mechanic        Mechanic -->|MCP<br/>Scan error codes| Scanner    Mechanic -->|MCP<br/>Look up repair steps| Manual    Mechanic -->|MCP<br/>Raise vehicle| Lift        style Manager fill:#ccf    style Mechanic fill:#9cf    style Supplier fill:#6cf    style Customer fill:#fc9    style Scanner fill:#ccc    style Manual fill:#ccc    style Lift fill:#ccc

  1. Customer Interaction (A2A): A customer communicates with the “Shop Manager” agent: “My car is making a rattling noise”

  2. Multi-turn Diagnosis (A2A): The Manager asks follow-up questions: “Can you send a video of the noise?” or “How long has that leaking been happening?”

  3. Internal Tool Usage (MCP): The Mechanic agent uses MCP to interact with:

    • Vehicle Diagnostic Scanner: scan_vehicle_for_error_codes(vehicle_id='XYZ123')
    • Repair Manual Database: get_repair_procedure(error_code, make, model)
    • Platform Lift: raise_platform(height_meters=2)

  4. Supplier Interaction (A2A): The Mechanic negotiates with a Parts Supplier agent: “Do you have part #12345 for a Toyota Camry 2018?”

This example shows how A2A and MCP work together seamlessly:

  • A2A handles higher-level, conversational, task-oriented interactions
  • MCP enables agents to use their specific, structured tools

Why Both Protocols?

The distinction matters because:

  • Tools are typically primitives with static, well-defined functions
  • Agents are autonomous systems that reason, plan, maintain state, and engage in complex dialogue

Trying to represent an agent as a simple tool fundamentally limits its capabilities. A2A respects the agent’s autonomy while enabling rich collaboration.

The A2A Request Lifecycle

When a client agent interacts with a remote A2A-enabled agent, it follows this sequence:

sequenceDiagram    participant Client as Client Agent    participant AuthSvr as Auth Server    participant A2ASvr as A2A Server<br/>(Remote Agent)        rect rgb(200, 255, 200)        Note over Client,A2ASvr: Agent Discovery        Client->>A2ASvr: GET /.well-known/agent-card.json        A2ASvr-->>Client: Return Agent Card<br/>(with security schemes)    end        rect rgb(200, 220, 255)        Note over Client,AuthSvr: Authentication        Client->>AuthSvr: Request token<br/>(authorizationUrl)        AuthSvr-->>Client: Return JWT Token    end        rect rgb(255, 230, 200)        Note over Client,A2ASvr: Send Message (Synchronous)        Client->>A2ASvr: POST /sendMessage<br/>with JWT + Message        A2ASvr-->>Client: Return Task Response<br/>(pending)    end        rect rgb(255, 200, 200)        Note over Client,A2ASvr: Send Message Stream (Asynchronous)        Client->>A2ASvr: POST /sendMessageStream<br/>with JWT        A2ASvr-->>Client: Task (Submitted)        A2ASvr-->>Client: TaskStatusUpdateEvent (Working)        A2ASvr-->>Client: TaskArtifactUpdateEvent (result chunk A)        A2ASvr-->>Client: TaskArtifactUpdateEvent (result chunk B)        A2ASvr-->>Client: TaskStatusUpdateEvent (Completed)    end
  1. Agent Discovery: Client obtains the Agent Card from a well-known URI or registry
  2. Authentication: Client requests a token from the auth server based on the Agent Card’s security schemes
  3. sendMessage API: Client sends a synchronous request and waits for a response
  4. sendMessageStream API: For long-running operations, client streams results via Server-Sent Events (SSE)

Conclusion

The A2A protocol represents a significant step forward in AI agent collaboration, providing the standardization and security needed for complex multi-agent systems. By combining A2A for agent-to-agent communication with MCP for tool integration, developers can build more powerful, flexible, and interoperable AI systems.

The future of AI lies not in isolated intelligent systems, but in orchestrated ecosystems of autonomous agents working together to solve complex problems.

理解 A2A 协议：代理间通信和代理发现

在快速发展的 AI 系统领域，Agent2Agent (A2A) 协议作为一个关键标准出现，使 AI 代理之间能够无缝通信和协作。本文探讨了什么是 A2A、代理发现如何工作，以及它如何与模型上下文协议 (MCP) 等其他协议相互补充。

什么是 A2A 协议？

A2A 协议是由 Linux 基金会开发的开放标准，可以实现 AI 代理之间的标准化通信和协作。与传统方法（将代理包装为工具）不同，A2A 允许代理直接作为自主实体进行交互，无论其底层框架或供应商如何。

A2A 解决的问题

考虑一个实际场景：规划国际旅行。这项任务需要协调多个专门的代理：

graph TD    subgraph before["没有 A2A：各自为政"]        UA1["Flight Agent"]        UA2["Hotel Agent"]        UA3["Currency Agent"]        UA4["Tour Agent"]        UA1 -.->|custom code| UA2        UA1 -.->|custom code| UA3        UA1 -.->|custom code| UA4        UA2 -.->|custom code| UA3        UA3 -.->|custom code| UA4        style UA1 fill:#ffcccc        style UA2 fill:#ffcccc        style UA3 fill:#ffcccc        style UA4 fill:#ffcccc    end        subgraph after["有 A2A：标准化协作"]        CA1["Flight Agent"]        CA2["Hotel Agent"]        CA3["Currency Agent"]        CA4["Tour Agent"]        CORE["A2A Protocol<br/>标准化通信"]        CA1 -->|A2A| CORE        CA2 -->|A2A| CORE        CA3 -->|A2A| CORE        CA4 -->|A2A| CORE        CORE --> CA1        CORE --> CA2        CORE --> CA3        CORE --> CA4        style CA1 fill:#ccffcc        style CA2 fill:#ccffcc        style CA3 fill:#ccffcc        style CA4 fill:#ccffcc        style CORE fill:#cce5ff    end        before -.->|统一标准| after
  • 航班预订代理
  • 酒店预订代理
  • 货币兑换代理
  • 本地旅游推荐代理

如果没有 A2A，集成这些多样化的代理会带来重大挑战：

  1. 代理暴露问题：开发人员经常将代理包装为工具，限制其能力并阻止直接的代理间协商
  2. 自定义集成：每次交互都需要定制的点对点解决方案
  3. 可扩展性问题：随着代理数量的增加，系统变得难以扩展和维护
  4. 互操作性缺陷：这限制了复杂 AI 生态系统的有机形成
  5. 安全顾虑：临时通信缺乏一致的安全措施

A2A 如何解决这些问题

A2A 通过建立一个标准化协议来解决这些挑战，使代理能够：

  • 可靠且安全地交互
  • 发现彼此的能力
  • 无需暴露内部实现细节就进行通信
  • 支持复杂的、多轮次的对话和协商
  • 在协作时保持自主性

A2A 的核心优势

  1. 安全协作：基于 HTTPS 的通信和一致的安全措施
  2. 互操作性：打破不同 AI 代理生态系统之间的壁垒
  3. 代理自主性：允许代理在协作时保持自身能力
  4. 降低集成复杂性：标准化通信减少工程开销
  5. 支持长时间运行的操作：原生支持异步执行和流式传输

关键设计原则

A2A 遵循企业级设计原则：

  • 简单性：利用现有标准如 HTTP、JSON-RPC 和服务器发送事件 (SSE)
  • 企业就绪：强大的身份验证、授权、安全性、隐私、追踪和监控
  • 异步：原生支持长时间运行的任务和流式传输
  • 模态独立：代理可以使用各种内容类型进行通信
  • 不透明执行：代理在不暴露内部逻辑的情况下协作，保护知识产权和安全性

代理发现：代理如何发现彼此

为了让 A2A 代理进行协作，它们首先需要发现彼此并理解其能力。这就是 Agent Card（代理卡） 的作用所在。

graph TB    Client["Client Agent<br/>需要发现其他代理"]        subgraph s1["Well-Known URI<br/>公开发现"]        Server1["Remote Agent Server"]        Card1["Agent Card<br/>/.well-known/<br/>agent-card.json"]        Server1 --> Card1        Client -->|GET| Card1        style Card1 fill:#ccffff        style Server1 fill:#e6f3ff    end        subgraph s2["Curated Registry<br/>企业目录发现"]        Registry["Central Registry"]        Cards["Agent Card<br/>Database"]        Registry --> Cards        Client -->|Query by Skill| Registry        Registry -->|Return matching| Client        style Registry fill:#ffe6cc        style Cards fill:#fff5e6    end        subgraph s3["Direct Config<br/>私有发现"]        Config["Config File<br/>Environment<br/>Variables"]        Client -->|Read| Config        style Config fill:#f0f0f0    end        Client -.-> s1    Client -.-> s2    Client -.-> s3

Agent Card：代理的数字名片

Agent Card 是一个 JSON 文档，作为 A2A Server（远程代理）的标准描述符。它包含：

  • 身份信息：名称、描述和提供商信息
  • 服务端点：A2A 服务的 URL
  • A2A 能力：支持的功能，如流式传输或推送通知
  • 身份验证：必需的安全方案（如 Bearer、OAuth2）
  • 技能：可用任务的描述，包括输入/输出模式和示例

发现策略

A2A 定义了三种主要的发现策略：

1. 众所周知的 URI（公开发现）

适用于：公开代理或域特定的发现

机制：

  • A2A Server 在标准位置托管其 Agent Card：https://{agent-server-domain}/.well-known/agent-card.json
  • 客户端代理对此端点执行 HTTP GET 请求
  • 服务器以 JSON 响应返回 Agent Card

优势：

  • 易于实现
  • 遵循 RFC 8615 标准
  • 便于自动发现

2. 精选注册表（基于目录的发现）

适用于：企业环境或公共市场

机制：

  • 中央注册表服务维护 Agent Card 的集合
  • A2A Server 将其卡发布到注册表
  • 客户端代理按技能、标签或其他标准查询注册表

优势：

  • 集中管理和治理
  • 基于能力的发现
  • 支持访问控制和信任框架

3. 直接配置（私有发现）

适用于：紧密耦合的系统或开发环境

机制：

  • 客户端应用程序通过配置文件或环境变量直接配置 Agent Card 信息

优势：

  • 对于已知的静态关系，实现简单

劣势：

  • 动态场景中灵活性不足
  • 需要客户端重新配置以进行更新

保护 Agent Cards

由于 Agent Card 可能包含敏感信息，A2A 建议：

  • 经过验证的 Agent Card：对敏感数据使用经过验证的扩展版本
  • 安全端点：通过 mTLS、IP 限制或 OAuth 2.0 实施访问控制
  • 注册表选择性披露：根据客户端身份和权限返回不同的卡
  • 无静态密钥：使用带外动态凭证而非嵌入式静态密钥

A2A 和 MCP：代理系统的互补协议

虽然 A2A 支持代理间的协作，但 Model Context Protocol (MCP) 关注代理如何与工具和资源交互。这些协议是互补的，而非竞争关系。

graph TB    subgraph A2A["A2A Protocol<br/>代理间协作"]        Agent1["Agent A<br/>自主系统<br/>可以推理、规划<br/>多轮对话"]        Agent2["Agent B<br/>自主系统<br/>可以推理、规划<br/>多轮对话"]        Agent1 <-->|协商<br/>委派任务| Agent2        style Agent1 fill:#ccffff        style Agent2 fill:#ccffff    end        subgraph MCP["MCP Protocol<br/>代理到工具"]        Agent["Agent<br/>智能系统"]        Tool1["Calculator"]        Tool2["Database API"]        Tool3["Weather Service"]        Agent -->|调用| Tool1        Agent -->|调用| Tool2        Agent -->|调用| Tool3        style Agent fill:#ffffcc        style Tool1 fill:#f0f0f0        style Tool2 fill:#f0f0f0        style Tool3 fill:#f0f0f0    end        A2A -->|互补协议| MCP        Note["<b>关键区别：</b><br/>A2A: 代理合作完成任务<br/>MCP: 代理使用工具"]    style Note fill:#fff,stroke:#000,stroke-width:2px

理解两者的区别

Model Context Protocol (MCP) 设计用于：

  • 将 AI 模型和代理连接到工具、API 和外部资源
  • 标准化代理如何调用无状态的、定义明确的函数
  • 典型用例：LLM 调用外部 API、代理查询数据库

Agent2Agent Protocol (A2A) 设计用于：

  • 使独立代理能够相互协作
  • 支持复杂的、多轮次的对话和协商
  • 典型用例：客户服务代理委派给计费代理、旅游代理与多个服务代理协调

关键区别

  • MCP：代理使用能力（工具）
  • A2A：代理在任务上合作伙伴

综合对比：API vs MCP vs A2A

以下是这三种方式的详细对比：

特性直接调用 APIMCP（工具协议）A2A（代理协议）
主体身份开发者（你）AI 模型（寻求工具）两个 AI Agent（互相协作/交易）
通信目标获取数据或结果访问特定本地/私有资源完成复杂的、跨主体的意图
灵活性低（代码写死）中（标准接口，随插随用）高（自主决策，动态博弈）
在区块链的应用简单的 Oracle 喂价AI 读取钱包私有账本意图驱动的资产结算

这个对比展示了为什么 A2A 对于复杂场景（如意图驱动的区块链应用）特别强大，其中多个自主代理需要进行协商和合作，而无需预先确定的逻辑。

真实案例：汽车维修店

想象一家配备 AI 代理”技工”的自动汽车维修店：

graph TB    Customer["Customer<br/>我的车有异响"]        subgraph UserAgent["User-Agent (A2A)"]        Manager["Shop Manager"]    end        subgraph AgentAgent["Agent-Agent (A2A)"]        Mechanic["Mechanic"]        Supplier["Parts Supplier"]    end        subgraph Tools["Agent-Tool (MCP)"]        Scanner["Vehicle Scanner"]        Manual["Repair Manual DB"]        Lift["Platform Lift"]    end        Customer -->|A2A对话<br/>多轮协商| Manager        Manager -->|A2A<br/>多轮问诊| Mechanic        Mechanic -->|A2A<br/>询问库存| Supplier    Supplier -->|A2A<br/>给出报价| Mechanic        Mechanic -->|MCP<br/>扫描错误码| Scanner    Mechanic -->|MCP<br/>查找维修步骤| Manual    Mechanic -->|MCP<br/>升起车辆| Lift        style Manager fill:#ccf    style Mechanic fill:#9cf    style Supplier fill:#6cf    style Customer fill:#fc9    style Scanner fill:#ccc    style Manual fill:#ccc    style Lift fill:#ccc

  1. 客户交互 (A2A)：客户与”店铺经理”代理通信：”我的汽车有异响”

  2. 多轮诊断 (A2A)：经理询问后续问题：”能发送异响的视频吗？”或”漏液多久了？”

  3. 内部工具使用 (MCP)：机械师代理使用 MCP 与以下工具交互：

    • 车辆诊断扫描仪：scan_vehicle_for_error_codes(vehicle_id='XYZ123')
    • 维修手册数据库：get_repair_procedure(error_code, make, model)
    • 升降平台：raise_platform(height_meters=2)

  4. 供应商交互 (A2A)：机械师与零件供应商代理协商：”你们有丰田凯美瑞 2018 款的零件 #12345 吗？”

这个例子展示了 A2A 和 MCP 如何seamlessly 协作：

  • A2A 处理更高层级的、对话式的、面向任务的交互
  • MCP 让代理使用其特定的、结构化的工具

为什么需要两个协议？

这些区别很重要，因为：

  • 工具通常是具有静态的、定义明确的功能的原语
  • 代理是自主系统，能够推理、规划、维护状态并进行复杂对话

试图将代理表示为简单工具从根本上限制了其能力。A2A 尊重代理的自主性，同时支持丰富的协作。

A2A 请求生命周期

当客户端代理与远程 A2A 代理交互时，它遵循以下顺序：

sequenceDiagram    participant Client as Client Agent    participant AuthSvr as Auth Server    participant A2ASvr as A2A Server<br/>(Remote Agent)        rect rgb(200, 255, 200)        Note over Client,A2ASvr: Agent Discovery        Client->>A2ASvr: GET /.well-known/agent-card.json        A2ASvr-->>Client: 返回 Agent Card<br/>(含安全方案信息)    end        rect rgb(200, 220, 255)        Note over Client,AuthSvr: Authentication        Client->>AuthSvr: 请求令牌<br/>(authorizationUrl)        AuthSvr-->>Client: 返回 JWT Token    end        rect rgb(255, 230, 200)        Note over Client,A2ASvr: Send Message (同步)        Client->>A2ASvr: POST /sendMessage<br/>with JWT + Message        A2ASvr-->>Client: 返回 Task Response<br/>(待处理)    end        rect rgb(255, 200, 200)        Note over Client,A2ASvr: Send Message Stream (异步)        Client->>A2ASvr: POST /sendMessageStream<br/>with JWT        A2ASvr-->>Client: Task (Submitted)        A2ASvr-->>Client: TaskStatusUpdateEvent (Working)        A2ASvr-->>Client: TaskArtifactUpdateEvent (结果片段A)        A2ASvr-->>Client: TaskArtifactUpdateEvent (结果片段B)        A2ASvr-->>Client: TaskStatusUpdateEvent (Completed)    end
  1. 代理发现：客户端从众所周知的 URI 或注册表获取 Agent Card
  2. 身份验证：客户端根据 Agent Card 的安全方案向身份验证服务器请求令牌
  3. sendMessage API：客户端发送同步请求并等待响应
  4. sendMessageStream API：对于长时间运行的操作，客户端通过服务器发送事件 (SSE) 流式传输结果

总结

A2A 协议代表了 AI 代理协作的重大进步,为复杂的多代理系统提供了所需的标准化和安全性。通过将 A2A 用于代理间通信，与 MCP 用于工具集成相结合，开发人员可以构建更强大、更灵活和更可互操作的 AI 系统。

AI 的未来不在于孤立的智能系统，而在于协调的自主代理生态系统一起工作来解决复杂问题。

entry.title: OpenClaw Meetup: AI Agent Engineering and Security Insights
entry.content:
OpenClaw Community Meetup Notes

I attended an OpenClaw offline meetup yesterday and learned a lot about the project’s latest progress, practical deployment experience, and LLM security risks. Here are the highlights.

1. OpenClaw Customization: Identity, Soul, and Scheduled Tasks

OpenClaw offers highly flexible customization for AI agents. You can define:

  • Identity for role and persona consistency
  • Soul for style and behavioral tone
  • Scheduled tasks for proactive execution

This allows agents to move beyond simple request-response patterns and become long-running assistants with stable personality and behavior.

Reference:

  • OpenClaw Meetup 2 Slides
  • OpenClaw Meetup Slides

2. Agora’s Voice Interaction Integration

A conventional voice AI pipeline usually goes through three steps:

  1. ASR (speech to text)
  2. LLM (text to text)
  3. TTS (text to speech)

Agora presented a more integrated approach by encapsulating most interaction interfaces and using OpenClaw APIs under the hood. This significantly reduces engineering overhead and improves end-user conversation flow.

3. Deployment Security: Default Settings Are Not Safe

One key warning from the meetup: default deployment settings are often unsafe. Automated bots can scan exposed services quickly, and serious incidents may happen within minutes, such as:

  • Unauthorized database access
  • Sensitive data leakage
  • Host compromise

Recommended hardening actions:

  1. Use SSH key authentication
  2. Configure firewall policies strictly
  3. Keep system and dependencies updated
  4. Enable Fail2ban to block brute-force attempts
  5. Isolate workloads with Docker

Reference:

  • ClawMacdo Deployment Security Toolkit

4. OWASP LLM Top 10 and Prompt Injection

OWASP’s LLM Top 10 risks discussed at the meetup:

  1. Prompt Injection
  2. Sensitive Information Disclosure
  3. Supply Chain Vulnerabilities
  4. Data and Model Poisoning
  5. Improper Output Handling
  6. Excessive Agency
  7. System Prompt Leakage
  8. Vector and Embedding Weaknesses
  9. Misinformation
  10. Unbounded Consumption

The highest-priority risk is Prompt Injection. Even when a model is instructed not to reveal secrets, attackers may still extract data through indirect and incremental prompts.

To reduce this risk, guardrail tools can provide policy checks, threat filters, and sensitive-output protection.

Reference:

  • SafeFence OpenClaw Guardrails

Summary

This meetup made one thing clear: capability growth and security discipline must go together in AI agent systems.

  • OpenClaw enables strong customization and extensibility
  • Integrated voice stacks can improve product experience significantly
  • Security hardening is mandatory, not optional
  • Prompt injection defense should be treated as a first-class requirement

“Security is not a feature, it is a continuous practice.”

OpenClaw 线下见面会记录

昨天参加了 OpenClaw 的线下见面会，收获很大。现场讨论了项目近期进展、工程落地方式，以及大模型安全风险。以下是我整理的核心内容。

1. OpenClaw 的定制能力：Identity、Soul 与定时任务

OpenClaw 提供了很强的智能体定制能力，可以定义：

  • Identity（身份）：保证角色一致性
  • Soul（风格）：塑造表达方式与行为倾向
  • 定时任务：让智能体具备主动执行能力

这意味着智能体不再只是被动问答工具，而是可以长期运行、具备稳定人格和行为逻辑的助手。

参考资料：

  • OpenClaw Meetup 2 Slides
  • OpenClaw Meetup Slides

2. Agora 的语音交互整合方案

传统语音 AI 流程通常要经过三步：

  1. ASR：语音转文字
  2. LLM：文字理解与生成
  3. TTS：文字转语音

Agora 在分享中展示了更一体化的方案：将大部分接口能力封装在平台层，并在底层调用 OpenClaw 接口。这样可以明显降低开发复杂度，也能提升最终对话体验。

3. 部署安全：默认配置并不安全

现场反复强调的一点是：默认部署配置往往不安全。暴露服务后，自动化 bot 会快速扫描，几分钟内就可能出现严重后果，例如：

  • 数据库被未授权访问
  • 敏感信息泄露
  • 主机被入侵

建议的加固措施包括：

  1. 使用 SSH Key 认证
  2. 严格配置防火墙策略
  3. 及时更新系统与依赖
  4. 启用 Fail2ban 防暴力破解
  5. 使用 Docker 进行服务隔离

参考资料：

  • ClawMacdo 部署安全工具

4. OWASP LLM Top 10 与 Prompt Injection

见面会讨论了 OWASP 的 LLM Top 10 风险：

  1. Prompt Injection（提示词注入）
  2. Sensitive Information Disclosure（敏感信息泄露）
  3. Supply Chain Vulnerabilities（供应链漏洞）
  4. Data and Model Poisoning（数据与模型投毒）
  5. Improper Output Handling（不当输出处理）
  6. Excessive Agency（代理权限过高）
  7. System Prompt Leakage（系统提示词泄露）
  8. Vector and Embedding Weaknesses（向量与嵌入弱点）
  9. Misinformation（错误信息）
  10. Unbounded Consumption（资源无限制消耗）

其中优先级最高的是 Prompt Injection。即便模型被指示“不要泄露敏感信息”，攻击者依然可以通过间接、拆分、递进式提问来套取信息。

要降低这类风险，建议引入 guardrails 工具，对输入输出做策略校验、风险过滤和敏感信息保护。

参考资料：

  • SafeFence OpenClaw Guardrails

总结

这次分享让我更明确一件事：AI 智能体系统的发展，必须把能力建设和安全治理放在同等优先级。

  • OpenClaw 的可定制性和扩展性很强
  • 一体化语音方案能显著提升产品体验
  • 部署加固是刚需，不是可选项
  • Prompt Injection 防御应作为一等工程问题

“安全不是一个功能点，而是持续的工程实践。”

entry.title: My Journey at Mistral AI Hackathon - BeWithMe Project
entry.content:
A Special Journey at Mistral AI Hackathon

Duration: February 28 - March 1, 2026

Last weekend, I joined the Mistral AI Hackathon in Singapore. Although my team did not win a prize, the experience was deeply rewarding, and I was honored to receive positive feedback from members of the Mistral AI technical team.

Project Overview: BeWithMe

Core Idea

BeWithMe is designed for people who need emotional companionship but cannot easily meet someone in person. Typical scenarios include:

  • Family members who live far away
  • Friends who are too busy to keep in touch
  • Loved ones who have passed away

Our goal is to use AI to provide a meaningful way to continue emotional connection.

Key Feature: AI Voice Cloning

The heart of the project is AI voice simulation:

  1. Users upload voice samples from loved ones
  2. The system fine-tunes models through deep learning
  3. The assistant responds using similar tone and speaking style
  4. Users get a virtual, emotionally familiar companion

Technical Architecture

Model Fine-Tuning

  • Google Colab for efficient model tuning with free GPU resources
  • Transfer learning based on open-source models

API Service Integration

  • Hugging Face Spaces for mature APIs such as Whisper
  • Avoiding heavy local inference deployment
  • Flexible orchestration of multiple pre-trained models

Data Persistence and Privacy

  • IPFS for storing conversation audio records
  • Blockchain for recording IPFS addresses and ensuring immutability
  • User-owned storage model with stronger data autonomy
  • Privacy-first control over sensitive content

Current Status

The design has been completed, and implementation is in progress. What I shared here reflects the initial architecture and partial implementation of BeWithMe.

Hackathon Takeaways

Technical Recognition

The Mistral AI technical team acknowledged innovation in:

  • Applying AI to real-world emotional support scenarios
  • Building practical systems with open-source and cloud tools
  • Combining technical feasibility with human-centered design

Network and Community

I connected with many talented developers and researchers in Singapore. The technical exchange and career conversations were highly valuable and may lead to future collaborations.

Broader Perspective

Working with teams from different backgrounds expanded my understanding of how AI can be turned into meaningful, real-world products.

Future Plan

  • Complete core product functions of BeWithMe
  • Improve speech quality and conversation naturalness
  • Strengthen privacy and security mechanisms
  • Explore more applications in emotional care scenarios

Acknowledgments

My sincere thanks to Mistral AI for organizing this excellent event, and to friends in Singapore for their warmth and support. This hackathon was not only a technical challenge, but also a memorable life experience.

I hope BeWithMe can eventually provide real support to people who need companionship.

“Technology should serve humanity, not the other way around.”

Mistral AI 黑客松的特别旅程

时间： 2026年2月28日 - 3月1日

上周末，我参加了在新加坡举办的 Mistral AI 黑客松。虽然最终没有获奖，但这次经历非常充实，也很荣幸获得了 Mistral AI 技术团队的积极评价。

项目介绍：BeWithMe

核心理念

BeWithMe 面向那些需要情感陪伴、但现实中难以经常见面的人群，典型场景包括：

  • 异地生活的家人
  • 因工作繁忙难以保持联系的朋友
  • 已经离世但仍被深深怀念的亲人

我们希望通过 AI 技术，提供一种更有温度的连接方式。

核心功能：AI 语音克隆

项目的关键能力是 AI 语音模拟：

  1. 用户上传亲友语音样本
  2. 系统通过深度学习进行微调
  3. 模型学习后可生成相似语气与表达风格
  4. 形成具有熟悉感的虚拟陪伴体验

技术架构

模型微调

  • 使用 Google Colab，利用免费 GPU 资源加速实验
  • 基于开源模型进行迁移学习

API 服务集成

  • 通过 Hugging Face Spaces 调用成熟能力（如 Whisper）
  • 避免复杂本地推理服务部署
  • 灵活编排多个预训练模型

数据持久化与隐私

  • 使用 IPFS 存储对话音频记录
  • 使用区块链保存 IPFS 地址，保证可追溯和不可篡改
  • 强化用户数据自主权
  • 以隐私保护为核心设计原则

当前进展

目前项目设计已完成，核心功能正在持续实现中。本文内容是 BeWithMe 的阶段性方案与部分落地结果。

黑客松收获

技术认可

Mistral AI 技术团队认可了项目在以下方面的创新性：

  • 将 AI 应用于真实的人文关怀场景
  • 用开源与云端工具实现高效系统组合
  • 在技术可行性与产品温度之间取得平衡

社区与人脉

我在现场结识了许多优秀开发者与研究者，围绕技术实现与职业发展进行了深入交流，这些连接对后续合作非常有价值。

视野拓展

与不同背景团队的互动，让我更清楚地看到 AI 从技术能力走向真实产品的路径。

后续计划

  • 完成 BeWithMe 的核心功能开发
  • 优化语音质量与对话自然度
  • 加强隐私与安全机制
  • 探索更多人文关怀方向的应用落地

致谢

感谢 Mistral AI 提供优质平台，也感谢新加坡朋友们的热情支持。这次黑客松不仅是技术挑战，更是一段非常珍贵的人生经历。

我希望 BeWithMe 最终能真正帮助到需要陪伴的人。

“技术应当服务于人，而不是反过来。”

entry.title: Joining Bybit as a QA Development Engineer
entry.content:
A New Chapter: Joining Bybit Singapore

Date: December 1, 2025
Location: Singapore
Position: QA Development Engineer

Today marks the beginning of an exciting new chapter in my career. I am thrilled to share that I have joined Bybit, one of the world’s leading cryptocurrency exchanges, as a QA Development Engineer in Singapore.

Why Bybit?

Bybit has long been at the forefront of blockchain and cryptocurrency innovation, recognized for both strong products and world-class engineering standards. With Singapore’s vibrant tech ecosystem and Bybit’s commitment to excellence, this role feels like the ideal next step.

As a QA Development Engineer, I will focus on:

  • Designing and developing automated testing frameworks
  • Building robust testing infrastructure for trading systems
  • Ensuring reliability and performance of blockchain applications
  • Collaborating with talented engineers across global teams

Expectations and Goals

This role combines my passion for AI, full-stack engineering, and quality assurance. I am especially looking forward to:

  1. Technical Growth

    • Mastering blockchain testing and QA methodologies
    • Building scalable automation frameworks
    • Gaining deep understanding of exchange systems

  2. Professional Development

    • Learning from experts in Web3 and fintech
    • Contributing to mission-critical systems serving millions of users
    • Expanding my network in the blockchain community

  3. Real-World Impact

    • Improving reliability of financial infrastructure
    • Helping secure user assets and transactions
    • Contributing to the future of decentralized finance

Gratitude and Excitement

I am deeply grateful for this opportunity and excited for what comes next. From my undergraduate thesis on blogging systems to full-stack development, AI research, and now blockchain, this journey has been both challenging and rewarding.

Special thanks to everyone who has supported me along the way, especially my mentors, teammates, and friends in Singapore.

What’s Next?

As I settle into this role, I will continue to:

  • Contribute to open-source projects
  • Share knowledge through blog posts and technical writing
  • Explore the intersection of AI and blockchain
  • Stay active in the tech community

More updates from my Bybit journey will be coming soon.

“Quality is not an act, it is a habit.” - Aristotle

新篇章：加入 Bybit 新加坡

日期： 2025年12月1日
地点： 新加坡
职位： QA 开发工程师

今天是我职业生涯中一个全新阶段的开始。我很高兴地和大家分享：我已经加入全球领先的加密货币交易所之一 Bybit，在新加坡担任 QA 开发工程师。

为什么选择 Bybit？

Bybit 一直处于区块链和加密货币技术创新前沿，以优秀的产品能力和高标准工程实践著称。结合新加坡活跃的技术生态，以及公司对卓越的坚持，这份工作非常契合我下一阶段的发展方向。

作为 QA 开发工程师，我将主要负责：

  • 设计与开发自动化测试框架
  • 为交易系统构建稳定可靠的测试基础设施
  • 保障区块链应用的可靠性与性能
  • 与全球优秀工程师团队紧密协作

期望与目标

这个角色融合了我对 AI、全栈工程和质量保障的长期兴趣。我尤其期待在以下方面持续成长：

  1. 技术成长

    • 掌握区块链测试与 QA 方法论
    • 构建可扩展的自动化测试体系
    • 深入理解交易所核心系统

  2. 职业发展

    • 向 Web3 与金融科技领域专家学习
    • 参与服务海量用户的关键系统建设
    • 拓展在区块链社区的人脉与合作机会

  3. 实际影响

    • 提升金融系统稳定性
    • 保护用户资产与交易安全
    • 为去中心化金融的长期发展贡献力量

感恩与期待

我非常珍惜这次机会，也对未来充满期待。从本科阶段研究博客系统，到全栈开发、AI 研究，再到今天进入区块链行业，这一路的积累让我受益匪浅。

感谢一路支持我的导师、同事和朋友，特别是在新加坡给予我帮助的伙伴们。

接下来

在适应新岗位的同时，我也会继续：

  • 参与开源项目建设
  • 通过博客和技术文章持续分享
  • 探索 AI 与区块链的结合点
  • 持续参与技术社区交流

后续我会继续更新在 Bybit 的工作与学习心得。

“质量不是一种行为，而是一种习惯。” - 亚里士多德
